options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: kingdom_sanity
options.description: Kingdoms must be found before they can be visited

options.name: run_type
options.description:
Determines which kingdoms can be visited during a run
Kingdom: Only base kingdoms will be visited
Extra: Only extra kingdoms will be visited
Either: Any kingdom can be visited determined by the starting route
Chaotic: Any kingdom can be visited in Chaotic Random fashion

options.name: max_kingdoms_per_run
options.description:
How many kingdoms you visit before heading to the pale keep or Reflecting Pool.
If progressive regions and/or kingdom sanity is on,
your run ends in a failure if you are not allowed to visit enough kingdoms.

options.name: progressive_regions
options.description:
Introduces items that increases the amount regions you are currently allowed to visit in a run.
Includes Pale Keep/Looping Hallway and Moonlit Pinnacle/Reflecting Pool.
Outskirts or Geode is always accessible.

options.name: excluded_kingdoms
options.description:
What kingdoms, if any, should not be added to the pool of locations.
There will always be a generic location that any class can obtain.
Available Options: [ "Scholar's Nest", "King's Arsenal", "Red Darkhouse", "Churchmouse Streets", "Emerald Lakeside",
                    "Darkhouse Depths", "Atelier Aurum", "Subterra Sanctum"]
Additionally you can exclude one of Kingdom Outskirts/Crack In The Geode, The Pale Keep/Looping Hallway,
and Moonlit Pinnacle/Reflecting Pool depending on your RunType

options.name: kingdom_sanity_kingdom_order
options.display_name: Enforce Kingdom Order With Kingdom Sanity
options.description:
Introduces another requirement to reaching a kingdom rather than just receiving the item.
You also need to have at least one kingdom from all the previous orders.
Kingdom Order is always used if you have Progressive Regions but not Kingdom Sanity,
so you can't access all kingdoms after receiving one progressive region.

options.name: kingdom_order
options.description:
Gates visiting a kingdom behind being able to reach the order
With Progressive Regions, the order directly ties to the number of progressive regions needed
With Kingdom Sanity, you must be able to visit at least one kingdom in each previous order
"0" will randomly assign it to an unfilled position if there are no valid positions unfilled,
it will place it in the last position that doesn't have more than the others.
Excluded kingdoms are not considered.
You will be able to choose any kingdom as your first kingdom in a route if logically accessible.
Throws an error if the number of 'valid' kingdoms is less than 'All Kingdoms'

options.name: class_sanity
options.description: Classes must be found before they can be played

options.name: exclude_class
options.description:
What class, if any, should not be accounted for in logic.
Throughout the run you will not be able to play that class at all.
Available Options: ["Wizard", "Assassin", "Heavyblade", "Dancer", "Druid", "Spellsword", "Sniper", "Bruiser",
                    "Defender", "Ancient", "Hammermaid", "Pyromancer", "Grenadier", "Shadow"]

options.name: checks_per_class
options.description:
What classes should have locations associated with them.
There will always be a generic location that any class can obtain.
Available Options: ["Wizard", "Assassin", "Heavyblade", "Dancer", "Druid", "Spellsword", "Sniper", "Bruiser",
                    "Defender", "Ancient","Hammermaid", "Pyromancer", "Grenadier", "Shadow"]
_ALL can be used to put checks on all classes

options.name: item_sanity
options.description:
What is required to have an item appear in a non AP treasuresphere
None: Any item is always available
Itemset: Receiving the itemset unlocks all the items in the itemset
Full: Need to get each individual item unlocked

options.name: checks_per_item_in_chest
options.description:
Each item in a chest is an archipelago item.
Shuffles treasure spheres into the item pool.
Does not add one per class if 'Checks Per Class' is on

options.name: upgrade_sanity
options.description:
What is required to have an upgrade appear in a shop, will randomly be chosen between available options still
None: Any upgrade is always available
Simple: Just need to get 'Ruby' item to unlock all the Ruby upgrades
Full: Need to get 'Ruby Primary' item to unlock the ruby upgrade for the primary

options.name: potion_sanity
options.description:
How the top shelf of the shop appears
None: Default behavior
Locked: Hp and level will be in their position when their item is found, and potions are added to the pool as they are found
Roulette: Randomly fills the slots with unlocked 'potions', meaning hp and level are not guaranteed

options.name: shop_sanity
options.description:
Shops contain ap items to purchase, ap item is required to purchase before you can purchase the normal shop item in that slot
None: Shops do not carry ap items
Global: Shops carry the same ap items, and only need to be purchased once to unblock that slot
Regional: Each kingdom contains their own ap items that need to be purchased

options.name: goal_condition
options.description:
Set your Goal Condition.
Shira: Defeat Shira x number of times, which is configured below

options.name: shira_defeats
options.description: How many unique classes must defeat Shira to goal if included in goal

options.name: witch_defeats
options.description: How many unique classes must defeat Witch to goal if included in goal

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
